name: Village
description: Clusters of houses, farms, and a meeting point. Home to villagers who trade items. Found in many biomes.
findMethod: Explore flat biomes like plains, savanna, or desert. Villages generate at the surface.

name: Pillager Outpost
description: Tall watchtower structure inhabited by pillagers. Killing the captain triggers Bad Omen.
findMethod: Found near villages in open biomes. Look for tall dark oak towers on the horizon.

name: Woodland Mansion
description: Massive dark oak mansion deep in dark forests. Contains evokers, vindicators, and rare loot.
findMethod: Buy a Woodland Explorer Map from a cartographer villager, or explore deep dark forests.

name: Ocean Monument
description: Large prismarine structure in deep ocean. Guarded by guardians and elder guardians. Source of sponges.
findMethod: Buy an Ocean Explorer Map from a cartographer villager, or search deep oceans.

name: Stronghold
description: Underground fortress containing the End Portal. Must be located using Eyes of Ender.
findMethod: Throw Eyes of Ender into the sky and follow them. They lead to the stronghold below.

name: Mineshaft
description: Branching wooden tunnels underground with rail tracks. Contains cave spiders and chests.
findMethod: Mine underground or explore caves. Badlands mineshafts generate at surface level.

name: Dungeon
description: Small cobblestone room underground with a monster spawner and loot chests.
findMethod: Listen for mob sounds underground. Dungeons are small rooms near caves.

name: Desert Pyramid
description: Sandstone pyramid with hidden treasure room and a TNT trap at the bottom.
findMethod: Search desert biomes. Pyramids are visible from the surface as sandstone structures.

name: Jungle Temple
description: Mossy cobblestone temple hidden in the jungle. Features arrow trap puzzles and a lever puzzle.
findMethod: Explore jungles carefully. Temples blend in with the thick foliage.

name: Swamp Hut
description: Small wooden hut on stilts inhabited by a witch. Contains a cauldron and a black cat.
findMethod: Explore swamp biomes. Small wooden huts visible on the water.

name: Igloo
description: Snow brick dome in snowy biomes. Some contain a secret basement with a zombie villager for curing.
findMethod: Search snowy plains and snowy taiga. Small snow domes partially buried in snow.

name: Ocean Ruins
description: Underwater stone or sandstone ruins. Cold ruins are made of stone bricks, warm ruins of sandstone.
findMethod: Swim or boat across oceans. Look for stone or sandstone structures on the ocean floor.

name: Shipwreck
description: Wrecked ship found in oceans or on beaches. Contains up to three loot chests.
findMethod: Explore oceans and beaches. Some shipwrecks are partially above water.

name: Buried Treasure
description: Single chest buried underground near beaches. Located using treasure maps from shipwrecks or ocean ruins.
findMethod: Find a treasure map in a shipwreck or ocean ruins. The X marks the spot on the map.

name: Ruined Portal
description: Partially destroyed Nether portal with a chest. Can appear anywhere in the Overworld or Nether.
findMethod: Found randomly across the Overworld surface and underground. Also generate in the Nether.

name: Ancient City
description: Massive deep underground structure in the Deep Dark. Home to the Warden. Contains rare loot and sculk.
findMethod: Mine deep below Y=-52. Found under mountain biomes. Bring wool to avoid triggering sculk shriekers.

name: Trail Ruins
description: Buried ancient settlement found underground. Contains suspicious gravel and sand for archaeology.
findMethod: Found underground in taiga and jungle biomes. Use a brush on suspicious gravel blocks.

name: Trial Chambers
description: Underground structure with trial spawners that scale to player count. Contains the Breeze mob and vaults.
findMethod: Found underground around Y=-20 to -40. Explore caves at deepslate level.

name: Nether Fortress
description: Dark brick fortress spanning Nether lava oceans. Source of blaze rods and nether wart.
findMethod: Explore the Nether. Fortresses generate in rows along north-south corridors.

name: Bastion Remnant
description: Massive blackstone structure inhabited by piglins and piglin brutes. Rich in gold loot.
findMethod: Explore the Nether. Found in all biomes except basalt deltas. Wear gold armor to avoid piglin aggro.

name: End City
description: Tall purpur towers in the outer End islands. End ships contain elytra. Guarded by shulkers.
findMethod: Defeat the Ender Dragon, then throw an ender pearl through the End Gateway portal.

name: End Gateway
description: Small bedrock portal that teleports players to the outer End islands or back to the main island.
findMethod: Generated after defeating the Ender Dragon. Throw an ender pearl into the beam.

name: Fossil
description: Large bone block structures found underground. Rare formations made of bone blocks and coal ore or deepslate diamond ore.
findMethod: Found underground in deserts, swamps, and mangrove swamps. Very rare.

name: Nether Fossil
description: Small bone block formations found in Soul Sand Valley. Unlike overworld fossils, these are classified as structures.
findMethod: Explore Soul Sand Valley in the Nether. Look for bone block formations near the surface.

name: Desert Well
description: Small sandstone structure with a water source. Contains suspicious sand for archaeology.
findMethod: Search desert biomes. Small sandstone wells with water visible at surface level.
